ArtifactCollectPolicy.description: Artifact collection policy applied during batch teardown.

oneOf.description: Export artifacts after every job.
Export artifacts only for successful jobs.
Export artifacts only for failed jobs.

BurstBufferConfig.description: Raw site-specific burst-buffer directives emitted into the batch script.

DependencyCondition.description: Normalized dependency conditions understood by the planner.

oneOf.description: Wait only until the upstream service is started.
Wait until the upstream service reports readiness.
Wait until the upstream service exits successfully.

EnvironmentSpec.description: Accepted environment syntaxes for service or prepare environments.

anyOf.description: Mapping form such as `{ FOO: bar }`.

ExecutionSpec.description: The final command form passed to the runtime container.

oneOf.description: Use the image's default entrypoint and command.
Run a shell-form command.
Run an exec-form argv vector.

HfStageKind.description: Whether an `hf://` stage-in source is a model or a dataset.

oneOf.description: A model repository (no `--repo-type`).
A dataset repository (`--repo-type dataset`).

HfStageSource.description:
A typed HuggingFace stage-in source.

The download runs cluster-side inside the Slurm allocation (the compute node
has network); hpc-compose never downloads on the laptop or over SSH. The
`revision` must be an immutable pin (commit SHA or explicit immutable tag);
floating refs are rejected at validation time.

HostMpiConfig.description: Host MPI installation bindings injected into a containerized MPI service.

ImageSource.description: Where a service image comes from after normalization.

oneOf.description: A local `.sqsh` or `.squashfs` file used directly at runtime.
A local Apptainer/Singularity `.sif` file used directly at runtime.
A remote image reference imported through Enroot.
No container image because the service runs on the host runtime.

JobDependencyCondition.description: Slurm job dependency condition for id-based dependencies.

oneOf.description: Start after the target job terminates in any state.
Start after the target job succeeds.
Start after the target job fails.

JobDependencyMode.description: Slurm dependency mode that does not require an explicit job id.

oneOf.description: Slurm singleton dependency.

anyOf.description: Shorthand job id form, equivalent to `afterany:<id>`.
Mapping form with an explicit condition.

MetricsCollector.description: Runtime metrics collector supported by the job sampler.

oneOf.description: Collect GPU telemetry through `nvidia-smi`.
Collect Slurm step metrics through `sstat`.
Collect sampled host CPU utilization through `/proc/stat`.

MpiConfig.description: First-class MPI launch configuration for one service step.

MpiImplementation.description: MPI implementation family used by a service image or host bind path.

oneOf.description: Open MPI.
MPICH.
Intel MPI.
MVAPICH2.
Cray MPI.
HPE MPI.
Implementation is intentionally unspecified.

MpiLauncher.description: MPI process launcher selected for the service.

oneOf.description: Slurm launches ranks directly with `srun --mpi=...`.

MpiProfile.description: MPI compatibility profile used for validation and diagnostics.

oneOf.description: Open MPI with Slurm PMI/PMIx launch.
MPICH with Slurm PMI/PMIx launch.
Intel MPI with Slurm PMI-2 launch.

MpiType.description: Slurm MPI plugin type used for `srun --mpi=<type>`.

NotifyEvent.description: Email lifecycle events supported by Slurm mail hooks.

oneOf.description: Send mail when the job begins executing.
Send mail when the job completes.
Send mail when the job fails.
Send mail when the job is requeued.
Send mail when the job cannot start due to an invalid dependency.
Send mail when burst-buffer stage-out completes.
Send mail when the job reaches its time limit.
Send mail when the job reaches 90% of its time limit.
Send mail when the job reaches 80% of its time limit.
Send mail when the job reaches 50% of its time limit.
Send mail once per array task (a modifier; requires `x-slurm.array`).
Use Slurm's `ALL` shorthand.

ParallelismConfig.description:
Descriptive tensor/pipeline parallelism geometry for one Slurm scope.

This is advisory metadata only. It emits no `#SBATCH`/`srun` flag and does
not change allocation or placement; it surfaces as the
`HPC_COMPOSE_TP_SIZE`/`HPC_COMPOSE_PP_SIZE` launch-environment variables and
is cross-checked against `gpus_per_node` at validation time.

PreparedImageSpec.description: A normalized image prepare block attached to a service.

oneOf.description: Wait for a fixed amount of time.
Poll a TCP port.

host.description: Optional host; defaults to localhost inside the job.

oneOf.description: Wait until a pattern appears in the service log.

pattern.description: Literal pattern to look for in the log.

oneOf.description: Poll an HTTP endpoint.

RendezvousClientConfig.description: Top-level client-side cross-job rendezvous discovery config.

RuntimeBackend.description: Runtime backend used to launch each service inside a Slurm step.

oneOf.description: Pyxis/Enroot through Slurm `srun --container-*` flags.
Apptainer SIF images launched through `apptainer exec/run`.
Singularity SIF images launched through `singularity exec/run`.
Host runtime without a container image.

RuntimeCacheCleanupPolicy.description:
Per-job runtime-cache cleanup policy applied in the batch teardown trap.

Controls whether the rendered `cleanup` trap removes the per-job enroot
runtime dirs (`$ENROOT_CACHE_PATH`/`$ENROOT_DATA_PATH`/`$ENROOT_TEMP_PATH`,
namespaced by `${SLURM_JOB_ID}`). Defaults to `Never` because
cancelled/crashed jobs never run the trap; host-side `clean`/`down` reaping
is the authoritative reclaimer.

oneOf.description: Leave the per-job runtime cache for host-side reaping (default).
Remove the per-job runtime cache only when the job exits successfully.
Remove the per-job runtime cache on every trap-reached exit path.

RuntimeGpuPolicy.description: GPU passthrough policy for container backends that need an explicit flag.

oneOf.description: Enable backend GPU passthrough when the job or service requests GPUs.
Do not add backend GPU passthrough flags.
Always use NVIDIA GPU passthrough flags for supported backends.

RuntimePlan.description: A plan with concrete runtime image paths for every service.

RuntimeService.description: A runtime-ready service entry with resolved image artifact paths.

ScratchCleanupPolicy.description: Scratch cleanup policy during batch teardown.

oneOf.description: Remove scratch on every exit path after stage-out.
Remove scratch only when the job exits successfully.
Leave scratch behind for manual inspection or site cleanup.

ScratchScope.description: Scratch storage scope requested for a job.

oneOf.description: Shared scratch path visible across all allocation nodes.
Node-local scratch path on each allocated node.

implicit.description:
`true` when the author omitted the condition (list-form `depends_on` or
mapping form without an explicit `condition:` key). Lint uses this to
recommend making the implicit `service_started` default explicit.

ServiceFailureMode.description: Per-service failure mode inside a single batch job.

oneOf.description: Any non-zero service exit fails the whole job.
Non-zero exits are recorded but do not fail the whole job.
Non-zero exits trigger bounded restarts before failing the job.

ServiceFailurePolicy.description: Normalized per-service failure policy with defaults resolved.

ServiceFailurePolicySpec.description: Raw per-service failure policy declaration under `x-slurm.failure_policy`.

oneOf.description: Run the hook in the generated batch-script supervisor on the host.
Run the hook inside the service container.

ServiceHookEvent.description: Event emitted by the service failure-policy supervisor.

oneOf.description: A failed service is about to be relaunched by `restart_on_failure`.
A failed service exceeded the rolling restart-window guard.

ServiceHookSpec.description:
Per-service prologue or epilogue hook.

YAML accepts either a string shorthand, which defaults to host execution, or
an object with explicit `context` and `script` fields.

script.description: Shell script body to run for this hook.

ServicePlacement.description: The effective `srun` placement geometry for one service.

allow_overlap.description: Whether this step is allowed to overlap another service placement.

exclude_indices.description: Zero-based allocation node indices excluded from this step.

mode.description: Placement mode used for this service.

node_indices.description: Zero-based allocation node indices selected for this step when known.

nodes.description: Number of nodes requested for the step.

ntasks.description: Explicit task count for the step when one is requested.

ntasks_per_node.description: Explicit tasks-per-node count for the step when one is requested.

pin_to_primary_node.description: Whether this step should be pinned to the primary node.

ServicePlacementMode.description: Service placement mode inside one Slurm allocation.

oneOf.description: The service is pinned to the allocation's primary node.
The service is pinned to an explicit subset of allocation nodes.
The service spans the full allocation.

ServicePlacementSpec.description: First-class service placement selector inside one Slurm allocation.

SignalName.description:
POSIX signal that Slurm's `--signal` may deliver to a job before its time
limit, restricted to the safe early-warning set.

oneOf.description: `SIGHUP` (1).
`SIGINT` (2).
`SIGQUIT` (3).
`SIGUSR1` (10) — the conventional checkpoint/preemption signal.
`SIGUSR2` (12).
`SIGTERM` (15).

Deliver straight to each service's job step (no directive prefix). The
application handles the signal itself; no trap is rendered.

Deliver only to the batch shell (`B:` prefix); a non-exiting forwarding
trap relays the signal to the running services.

SlurmConfig.description: Top-level `x-slurm` configuration shared by all services.

enroot_temp_dir.description:
Optional override for enroot's prepare-time temporary extraction scratch
directory (`ENROOT_TEMP_PATH`), separate from the persistent
[`Self::cache_dir`]. Point it at fast node-local storage (e.g.
`/tmp/${USER}-hpc-compose-enroot`) when the shared cache filesystem causes
`Stale file handle` errors during image import; the final image and layer
cache still live under `cache_dir`. Interpolated; defaults to
`<cache_dir>/enroot/tmp` when unset.

runtime_root.description:
Optional override for the directory that holds per-job runtime state
(`<runtime_root>/<job_id>/{logs,metrics,state.json,artifacts}`). Defaults
to `<submit_dir>/.hpc-compose`. Relative values resolve against the submit
directory. Must be visible from both login and compute nodes.

StageInConfig.description:
Stage-in path mapping run before service launch.

Either a filesystem `from` path or a typed `hf://` source (`hf`) is staged
into `to`; exactly one of the two must be set.

StageMode.description: File transfer implementation for stage-in and stage-out.

oneOf.description: Use `rsync -a`.
Use portable `cp -R`/`cp`.

StageOutConfig.description: Stage-out path mapping run during batch teardown.

StageOutWhen.description: Stage-out policy for one path mapping.

oneOf.description: Stage out on every exit path.
Stage out only when the job exits successfully.
Stage out only when the job fails.
Surface an advisory in status/watch/stats output.
Reserved for a future runtime-enforced self-cancel policy.

WatchdogResourceConfig.description: Per-resource thresholds for the idle-resource watchdog.
